source_note: Synthesized APQC PCF v8.0 + EFQM 2025 unified taxonomy (L1/L2) with APQC L3 processes attached; scoring grain L2, L3 optional drill-down. v2: orientation tags (internal|external) per item for SWOT derivation; L3 inherits from parent.

categories.title: Strategy, Purpose & Governance
categories.definition: How the organisation sets its purpose and direction, and how leadership governs strategy, structure, and performance.

items.title: Define purpose, vision, mission, and long-term direction
items.definition: Clarity and communication of why the organisation exists and where it is going long term.

children.title: Survey market and determine customer needs and wants

items.title: Understand the ecosystem, market, capabilities, and major challenges
items.definition: How well leadership understands its markets, competitive ecosystem, internal capabilities, and the challenges ahead.

items.title: Identify and understand key stakeholder needs and expectations
items.definition: How systematically stakeholder needs and expectations are identified and reflected in decisions.

items.title: Develop and adapt enterprise and business-unit strategy
items.definition: Quality and adaptiveness of enterprise and business-unit strategy development.

children.title: Define and evaluate strategic options to achieve the mission
Coordinate and align cross-functional and process strategies

items.title: Develop strategic initiatives and transformation priorities
items.definition: How strategic initiatives and transformation priorities are defined, funded, and sequenced.

children.title: Refine strategic initiatives and project plans as needed

items.title: Design and maintain business models
items.definition: How deliberately the business model is designed, tested, and kept current.

items.title: Design organisational structure and governance
items.definition: Fitness of organisational structure, decision rights, and governance arrangements.

items.title: Establish performance management and strategic review systems
items.definition: Rigor of performance management and the cadence of strategic review and course correction.

categories.title: Products, Services & Sustainable Value
categories.definition: How offerings are conceived, developed, launched, and managed to create lasting value for customers and society.

items.title: Govern product and service portfolios and lifecycles
items.definition: Portfolio-level governance of products and services across their lifecycles.

items.title: Generate and define new product and service concepts
items.definition: Strength of the pipeline for generating and defining new product and service concepts.

children.title: Manage product and service life cycle
Manage patents, copyrights, and regulatory requirements
Manage product and service master data

items.title: Develop, test, launch, and improve products and services
items.definition: Effectiveness of development, testing, launch, and post-launch improvement.

children.title: Design and prototype products and services
Test market for new or revised products and services

items.title: Design how sustainable value is created and delivered
items.definition: How the organisation designs offerings to create sustainable economic, social, and environmental value.

items.title: Manage product, service, warranty, and regulatory requirements
items.definition: Management of product/service requirements, warranties, and regulatory conformity.

categories.title: Marketing, Sales & Customer Growth
categories.definition: How the organisation understands markets, builds its brand, sells effectively, and grows customer relationships.

items.title: Understand markets, customers, and growth opportunities
items.definition: Depth of market, customer, and opportunity insight informing growth choices.

children.title: Perform customer and market intelligence analysis

items.title: Develop brand, positioning, pricing, and channel strategy
items.definition: Coherence of brand, positioning, pricing, and channel strategy.

items.title: Develop and manage marketing plans and communications
items.definition: Quality and impact of marketing planning and communications.

children.title: Define offering and customer value proposition
Design and manage customer loyalty program
Establish goals, objectives, and measures for products/services by channel/segment
Analyze and respond to customer insight
Develop and manage sales proposals, bids, and quotes

items.title: Develop sales strategy, forecasts, goals, and budgets
items.definition: Discipline of sales strategy, forecasting, target setting, and budgeting.

items.title: Manage leads, opportunities, accounts, proposals, and sales
items.definition: Effectiveness of pipeline management from lead through proposal to closed sale.

items.title: Manage customer loyalty, retention, and growth
items.definition: How well customer loyalty, retention, and account growth are cultivated.

categories.title: Supply Chain, Procurement & Logistics
categories.definition: How materials and services are planned, sourced, produced, and moved — and how the supply chain stays resilient.

items.title: Plan and align supply chain resources, demand, and capacity
items.definition: Alignment of supply chain resources and capacity with demand plans.

children.title: Create and manage master production schedule

items.title: Source and procure materials and services
items.definition: Effectiveness and economics of sourcing and procurement.

items.title: Manage suppliers, partners, contracts, and categories
items.definition: Strength of supplier, partner, contract, and category management.

items.title: Produce, assemble, test, and assure product quality
items.definition: Reliability of production, assembly, testing, and quality assurance.

children.title: Maintain production records and manage lot traceability

items.title: Manage warehousing, inventory, transportation, and logistics
items.definition: Efficiency of warehousing, inventory, transportation, and logistics.

items.title: Manage supply chain resilience and continuity
items.definition: Preparedness of the supply chain for disruption, and speed of recovery.

children.title: Plan and manage inbound material flow

categories.title: Service Delivery & Operations
categories.definition: How services are planned, resourced, operated, and delivered reliably to customers day to day.

items.title: Establish service delivery governance and strategy
items.definition: Clarity of governance and strategy for service delivery.

items.title: Plan and manage service delivery resources and capacity
items.definition: Planning of service delivery resources and capacity against demand.

items.title: Operate and assure service delivery systems
items.definition: Stability and assurance of the systems that run service operations.

items.title: Deliver services to customers
items.definition: Consistency and quality of day-to-day service delivery to customers.

items.title: Manage operational performance and continuous improvement
items.definition: Use of operational metrics and continuous improvement in service operations.

categories.title: Customer Service & Experience
categories.definition: How customer inquiries, issues, and post-sale needs are handled, and how the customer experience is measured and improved.

items.title: Develop customer service and experience strategy
items.definition: Existence and clarity of a customer service and experience strategy.

children.title: Define customer service requirements across the enterprise
Define and manage customer service channel strategy
Define customer service policies and procedures
Establish target service level for each customer segment
Identify and define customer service strategy integrations
Measure customer satisfaction with customer problems, requests, and inquiries handling
Measure customer satisfaction with customer-complaint handling and resolution
Measure customer satisfaction with products and services

items.title: Plan and manage customer service channels and workforce
items.definition: Planning of service channels and the service workforce.

children.title: Plan and manage customer service workforce
Manage customer service problems, requests, and inquiries
Report incidents and risks to regulatory bodies

items.title: Manage customer inquiries, requests, complaints, and returns
items.definition: Handling of inquiries, requests, complaints, and returns.

children.title: Assess the likelihood and consequences of occurrence of any hazards

items.title: Provide post-sale service, warranty, and support
items.definition: Quality of post-sale service, warranty fulfilment, and support.

items.title: Measure and improve customer experience and perceptions
items.definition: Measurement of customer experience and how findings drive improvement.

categories.title: People, Culture & Leadership
categories.definition: How the organisation attracts, develops, and retains people — and how culture, leadership, and engagement are built.

items.title: Develop workforce strategy, policies, and plans
items.definition: Quality of workforce strategy, policies, and workforce planning.

children.title: Develop and implement workforce planning, policies, and strategies
Monitor and update HR strategies, plans, and policies

items.title: Recruit, onboard, deploy, and retain people
items.definition: Effectiveness of recruiting, onboarding, deployment, and retention.

children.title: Develop and manage reward, recognition, and motivation programs

items.title: Develop capabilities, learning, careers, and succession
items.definition: Investment in capability building, learning, careers, and succession.

items.title: Manage performance, rewards, recognition, and wellbeing
items.definition: Fairness and effectiveness of performance management, rewards, recognition, and wellbeing.

children.title: Develop and manage time and attendance systems
Manage/Collect employee suggestions and perform employee research

items.title: Shape organisational culture and nurture core values
items.definition: Strength and consistency of organisational culture and lived values.

items.title: Develop leadership and engage people in purpose and strategy
items.definition: Leadership development and how well people are engaged in purpose and strategy.

items.title: Create conditions for change, agility, creativity, and innovation
items.definition: Conditions for change-readiness, agility, creativity, and innovation in the workforce.

categories.title: Technology, Data & Innovation
categories.definition: How technology, data, and innovation are governed, operated, protected, and used to transform the business.

items.title: Develop and manage technology strategy and governance
items.definition: Quality of technology strategy and IT governance.

children.title: Develop and manage IT service levels
Define business technology and governance strategy
Develop IT compliance, risk, and security strategy
Control IT risk, compliance, and security
Develop and manage IT security, privacy, and data protection
Conduct and analyze IT compliance assessments
Develop and execute IT resilience and continuity operations
Manage IT user identity and authorization
Execute IT service/solution creation and testing

items.title: Manage technology services, applications, and infrastructure
items.definition: Reliability and efficiency of technology services, applications, and infrastructure.

children.title: Develop and manage service/solution deployment strategy

items.title: Manage information, data, knowledge, and analytics
items.definition: Management of information, data, knowledge, and analytics as enterprise assets.

children.title: Define business information and analytics strategy
Define and maintain business information architecture
Define and execute business information lifecycle planning and control

items.title: Manage cybersecurity, privacy, and technology risk
items.definition: Strength of cybersecurity, privacy protection, and technology risk management.

items.title: Drive digital transformation and technology-enabled innovation
items.definition: Progress and ambition of digital transformation and technology-enabled innovation.

items.title: Evaluate and adopt emerging technologies
items.definition: Discipline in evaluating and adopting emerging technologies.

children.title: Define and establish service delivery strategy
Define and develop service support strategy
Plan and manage service delivery control
Develop and manage infrastructure resource planning
Develop and manage service delivery operations

categories.title: Finance & Enterprise Performance
categories.definition: How financial performance is planned, managed, reported, and translated into enterprise value.

items.title: Plan, budget, forecast, and manage financial performance
items.definition: Rigor of planning, budgeting, forecasting, and management accounting.

children.title: Develop and manage financial resources strategy

items.title: Manage revenue, receivables, credit, and collections
items.definition: Management of revenue, receivables, credit, and collections.

items.title: Manage accounting, close, consolidation, and reporting
items.definition: Quality of accounting operations, close, consolidation, and reporting.

items.title: Manage treasury, cash, capital, and funding
items.definition: Effectiveness of treasury, cash, capital, and funding management.

children.title: Perform capital planning and project approval

items.title: Manage tax and statutory financial obligations
items.definition: Management of tax and statutory financial obligations.

children.title: Monitor and execute risk and hedging transactions
Establish internal controls, policies, and procedures
Operate controls and monitor compliance with internal controls policies and procedures

items.title: Measure strategic and operational performance
items.definition: Measurement of strategic and operational performance against targets.

items.title: Manage economics, financial outcomes, and value realization
items.definition: Translation of financial management into economic outcomes and realized value.

categories.title: Assets & Enterprise Resources
categories.definition: How physical assets, facilities, and enterprise resources are acquired, operated, maintained, and optimized.

items.title: Plan, acquire, construct, and deploy assets
items.definition: Planning, acquisition, construction, and deployment of assets.

children.title: Develop property strategy and long term vision

items.title: Operate, maintain, and optimize physical assets
items.definition: Operation, maintenance, and optimization of physical assets.

items.title: Manage facilities, property, and workplace resources
items.definition: Management of facilities, property, and workplace resources.

items.title: Manage asset lifecycle, disposal, and retirement
items.definition: Lifecycle management of assets through disposal and retirement.

children.title: Perform waste and hazardous goods management

items.title: Optimize enterprise resource utilization
items.definition: Optimization of utilization across enterprise resources.

categories.title: Risk, Compliance & Resilience
categories.definition: How risk, compliance, controls, continuity, and sustainability obligations are managed to protect the enterprise.

items.title: Manage enterprise, strategic, operational, and financial risk
items.definition: Management of enterprise, strategic, operational, and financial risk.

children.title: Establish the enterprise risk framework and policies
Oversee and coordinate enterprise risk management activities
Manage business unit and function risk

items.title: Manage compliance, ethics, legal, and regulatory obligations
items.definition: Management of compliance, ethics, legal, and regulatory obligations.

items.title: Manage audit, controls, assurance, and remediation

items.definition: Preparedness for business continuity, crisis response, and resilience.

children.title: Share knowledge of specific risks across other parts of the organization

items.title: Manage environmental, social, and sustainability obligations
items.definition: Management of environmental, social, and sustainability obligations.

items.title: Manage partner, alliance, and ecosystem relationships
items.definition: Health of partner, alliance, and ecosystem relationships.

items.definition: Engagement between management, the board, and key external stakeholders.

items.definition: Management of business capabilities and the operating model.

items.definition: Management of change and organisational transformation.

items.definition: Management of knowledge, learning, and organisational maturity.
